The file has already been loaded via the system reminder above. Here's a summary:

**proactive-context** is a local-first RAG system (v0.1, implemented) that indexes markdown files and provides semantic search + LLM-powered Q&A, with no external infrastructure required.

**Core commands:**
- `init` — starts a background file watcher/indexer (idempotent, safe to re-run)
- `query` — semantic search with optional reranking
- `generate` — parallel fan-out retrieval + LLM synthesis via OpenRouter/rig
- `stats` / `status` — index health and daemon status

**Key design decisions:**
- Embeddings via `fastembed` (local ONNX, no GPU needed)
- Vector storage in `sqlite-vec` (`index.db` per project)
- `generate` decomposes queries into sub-queries, runs retrieval in parallel, prefetches full source docs, then hands rich context to a single `rig` agent with a `read_file` tool
- Config at `~/.proactive-context/config.json`

**Main limitations:**
- Naive character-based chunking
- Markdown-only
- `generate` requires OpenRouter key (no local LLM path)
- One daemon per directory

Is there something specific you'd like to do with this project?

here's what I'm thinking: what if we were to also index everything the user ever says (this "generate" tool will be called every time I use claude code in any project)

what if we keep track of every conversation the user is participating and we start learning lessons and guarding whatever the user is saying and we include that information in whatever the user is saying when we inject context proactively? thoughts?

This is a genuinely good idea. Let me think through the two distinct problems embedded in it:

**1. Conversation capture + indexing** — straightforward extension of the existing infra. Conversations are just text; you'd chunk and embed them the same way. The tricky part is *collection*: Claude Code writes conversation logs to `~/.claude/projects/*/`, so a watcher on that directory could pick them up automatically, same as the markdown watcher today.

**2. Lesson extraction + proactive injection** — the harder, more valuable half. Raw conversation chunks retrieved by similarity are noisy. The better pattern is a second async pass: after a session ends, run a cheap LLM call to distill the session into structured lessons ("user prefers X", "this project uses Y", "user rejected Z approach and why"). Those distilled notes are what you index and retrieve from.

For injection, Claude Code has **hooks** — specifically `UserPromptSubmit` fires before every user message. You could wire a hook that runs `proactive-context query <current prompt>` and prepends the top hits as a system-level context block. That's the "proactive" part of the name actually earning its keep.

**The real design question** is whether to treat conversations as a separate corpus or fold them into the same `index.db`. I'd argue separate — different chunking strategy, different metadata (timestamp, project, session ID), and you may want to query them independently.

**Main risks:**
- Lessons from 6 months ago that are now wrong get confidently injected as truth
- Conversation logs contain sensitive project context you may not want cross-contaminating other projects
- The injection hook adds latency to every prompt

Worth building? Yes. The existing infra covers ~60% of it already. Want to sketch a concrete implementation plan?
add a justfile that installs it in my ~/.bin or ~/bin or something like that that's in my $PATH and run it
Done. `proactive-context` is now in `~/.bin/` and ready to use. The justfile has a single `install` recipe — run `just install` any time you want to rebuild and redeploy.
is there a way to see ingestion in real time? like a stats --watch

No `--watch` flag exists yet — `stats` is a one-shot print. The product spec mentions it as a desired feature but it's not implemented.

You can approximate it right now with:

```
watch -n 1 proactive-context stats
```

That refreshes every second. Want me to implement a proper `stats --watch` that uses a live in-place terminal update (clearing and redrawing the line counts as they grow)?
add it -- make it look good and colorize stats too

Working. The `[2J[H` and `[H` are the ANSI escape codes that clear/position the cursor — in a real terminal they're invisible and just move the cursor, so successive draws overwrite each other cleanly with no flicker.

Summary of what was added:
- `proactive-context stats` now shows colorized output with daemon status (green ● / red ●), file/chunk counts, DB size, and embedding model+dim
- `proactive-context stats --watch` refreshes every second in-place (cursor reposition, no clear flicker)
- `daemon_pid()` helper in daemon.rs, `IndexStats` struct + `index_stats_full()` in db.rs
Done — `~/.bin/proactive-context` is up to date and `~/.bin` is already in your `$PATH`.
